content:
# Porous Media

**Summary**: A material containing pores, such as rock or soil, used to model fluid flow in reservoirs.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] modeled the movement of fluids through a porous-medium.

- [[2013-khan-2|Khan, 2013]] studied the behavior of slightly compressible fluids in porous media using fractional diffusion.

- [[2014-jamil|JAMIL, 2014]] modeled the flow of CO2 through porous media to account for viscous and inertial resistances.

- [[2015-alloush|Alloush, 2015]] studied gas flow in porous media using modified diffusivity equations.

- [[2016-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2016]] investigated foam behavior within a sandstone reservoir model.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] used fly ash as a porous medium to act as an adsorbent for cadmium removal.

- [[2018-elsaieed|ElSaieed, 2018]] modeled flow through porous media using Darcy's law and Stokes-Brinkman equations.

- [[2018-jamal|Jamal, 2018]] modeled the interaction between free-flow zones and porous media using the Brinkman equation.

- [[1992-al-towailib-2|Al-Towailib, 1992]] analyzed two-phase flow through chokes as a porous-media problem.

## Scientific Context

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] The primary medium for oil and gas transport in the earth's crust.

- [[2013-khan-2|Khan, 2013]] studied the behavior of slightly compressible fluids in porous media using fractional diffusion.

- [[2014-jamil|JAMIL, 2014]] modeled the flow of CO2 through porous media to account for viscous and inertial resistances.

- [[2015-alloush|Alloush, 2015]] studied gas flow in porous media using modified diffusivity equations.

- [[2016-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2016]] investigated foam behavior within a sandstone reservoir model.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] used fly ash as a porous medium to act as an adsorbent for cadmium removal.

- [[2018-elsaieed|ElSaieed, 2018]] modeled flow through porous media using Darcy's law and Stokes-Brinkman equations.

- [[2018-jamal|Jamal, 2018]] modeled the interaction between free-flow zones and porous media using the Brinkman equation.

- [[1992-al-towailib-2|Al-Towailib, 1992]] analyzed two-phase flow through chokes as a porous-media problem.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013]] Characterized by porosity and permeability.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The displacement model was simplified for one-dimensional porous-media under low pressure gradients. ([[2013-al-mutairi|AL-MUTAIRI, 2013, p. 58]])


- The model accounts for anomalous diffusion within these systems. ([[2013-khan-2|Khan, 2013, p. 101]])

- Bed porosity was found to influence peak temperature values and timing within the tank. ([[2014-jamil|JAMIL, 2014, p. 19]])

- The study highlights that standard Darcy's law is insufficient for high-velocity flows in these systems. ([[2015-alloush|Alloush, 2015, p. 18]])

- Not stated in source. ([[2016-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2016]])

- Fly ash is a waste product with high surface area suitable for heavy metal adsorption. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 25]])

- The transition of flow from free-flow regions to porous media is a critical area for modeling accuracy. ([[2018-elsaieed|ElSaieed, 2018, p. 20]])

- Tracer transport in porous media is slower due to lower permeability and adsorption on rock surfaces. ([[2018-jamal|Jamal, 2018, p. 86]])

- The correlation accounts for the complexities of multi-phase flow in choke systems. ([[1992-al-towailib-2|Al-Towailib, 1992, p. 96]])
